During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|12=draft}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏|12=draft}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日|12=draft}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|12=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|12=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|12=draft}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|12=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|12=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ|lt=Tamayoribime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|ja|米子駅|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|12=draft}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|12=draft}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|12=draft}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = {{ill|Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg|ja|Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg|12=draft}} |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft}} 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=draft}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft}} |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|12=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊|12=draft}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|12=draft}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|12=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|12=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|12=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. {{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = {{ill|Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg|ja|Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg|12=draft}} |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft}} 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=draft}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft}} |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|12=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊|12=draft}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|12=draft}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏|12=draft}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日|12=draft}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|12=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|12=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|12=draft}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|12=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|12=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ|lt=Tamayoribime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|ja|米子駅|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|12=draft}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|12=draft}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|12=draft}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社]]

In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ|lt=Tamayoribime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|ja|米子駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|12=draft|12=draft}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|12=draft|12=draft}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|12=draft|12=draft}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = {{ill|Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg|ja|Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg|12=draft|12=draft}} |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=draft|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊|12=draft|12=draft}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|12=draft|12=draft}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=draft|12=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|12=draft|12=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=draft|12=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=draft|12=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|12=draft|12=draft}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ|lt=Tamayoribime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|ja|米子駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|12=draft|12=draft}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|12=draft|12=draft}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|12=draft|12=draft}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qq]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社]]

Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=draft|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊|12=draft|12=draft}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|12=draft|12=draft}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=draft|12=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|12=draft|12=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=draft|12=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=draft|12=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|12=draft|12=draft}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ|lt=Tamayoribime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|ja|米子駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|12=draft|12=draft}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=draft|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊|12=draft|12=draft}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|12=draft|12=draft}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=draft|12=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|12=draft|12=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=draft|12=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=draft|12=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|12=draft|12=draft}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社

Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|12=draft|12=draft}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|12=draft|12=draft}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qq]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=draft|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊|12=draft|12=draft}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|12=draft|12=draft}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=draft|12=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|12=draft|12=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=draft|12=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=draft|12=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|12=draft|12=draft}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ|lt=Tamayoribime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|ja|米子駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|12=draft|12=draft}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊|12=draft|12=draft}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=draft|12=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|12=draft|12=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=draft|12=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=draft|12=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|12=draft|12=draft}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社

Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=draft|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊|12=draft|12=draft}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|12=draft|12=draft}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=draft|12=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|12=draft|12=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=draft|12=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=draft|12=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|12=draft|12=draft}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
