Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|12=draft}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|12=draft}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町}} 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} 5), the unRanked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村}}—and the unRanked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} 6), the unRanked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村}}) and the unRanked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ|lt=Tamayoribime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|ja|米子駅}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]]

In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ|lt=Tamayoribime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|ja|米子駅}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町}} 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}} |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ|lt=Tamayoribime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|ja|米子駅}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 賀茂神社 |画像 = [[File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]倭3 |位置 = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[大社造]] |別名 = 賀茂大明神
賀茂両社
賀茂下上大明神 |例祭 = [[10月19日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label=賀茂神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''賀茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は[[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]倭にある[[神社]]。 旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}。 == 由緒 == 創立年代不詳。京都[[賀茂別雷神社]]より[[分霊|御分霊]]を奉遷して大平山（おおなるやま）の頂上に鎮座していたと云われる。その後、同山の中腹小平という所に遷り、さらに同山の麓に奉遷したと伝えられる{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]、[[尼子経久]]が[[伯耆国]]を領有すると、崇敬篤く社領100石が寄附された他、度々参拝して祈願、奉幣、社殿造営が行われたが、[[尼子氏]]滅亡と共に当社も次第に衰微した{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}。[[江戸時代]]の[[1733年]]（[[享保]]18年）[[鳥取藩]]主[[池田氏#鳥取藩主家|池田氏]]より社領高10石の寄進があり、[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）4月郷社に列せられ、[[1907年]]（明治40年）[[2月3日]]に[[近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|神饌幣帛料供進社]]に指定された{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}。猪小路、北方、原、小原、新庄、上阿賀、下阿賀7ヶ村の氏神であり、近世まで賀茂大明神と称した{{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}。 [[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）5月に[[大国村 (鳥取県)|大国村]]大字北方字宮ノ峠ノ山鎮座の[[近代社格制度#諸社|無格社]]北方神社、同村大字原字客ノ前鎮座の無格社小原神社、[[1917年]]（大正6年）[[4月30日]]に[[天津村 (鳥取県)|天津村]]大字阿賀字王子﨏山鎮座の無格社阿賀神社、同村大字同字トヲベン神田鎮座の無格社田邊神社を[[神社合祀|合併]]した{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}。 === 境内社 === '''糺神社'''（ただすじんじゃ）は本社と同時の創立と思われ、本社と併せて'''賀茂両社'''、あるいは'''賀茂下上大明神'''と称されていた{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}。 '''鷺神社'''（さぎじんじゃ）の創立年代は不詳{{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}。もと[[雲伯]]の国境渡田峠、通称疱瘡ヶ峠と称する所に鎮座していた。古来[[疱瘡神]]として信仰篤く、旧藩主池田氏の祈祷所であったと伝えられる{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}。[[1897年]]（明治30年）10月失火により社殿が焼失したが[[1905年]]（明治38年）5月に再建された。1916年（大正5年）猪小路鎮座の田中神社（祭神：[[イワナガヒメ|磐長姫命]]）を合併し、[[1917年]]（大正6年）に渡田峠より現在地の賀茂神社境内に奉遷した{{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}。 もとの場所には小祠があり祠内に疱瘡地蔵が祀られ{{Sfn|石破|2000|p=30}}、小祠の前には[[1825年]]（[[文政]]8年）6月の御神燈が残されている{{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}。現在の祭神は稲背脛命であるが『伯耆志』に「祭神は[[中山神社_(鳥取県大山町)#境内社|束積の鷺神社]]に同じ」とあるため、もとの祭神は[[スサノオ]]であった可能性がある{{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}。 == 祭神 == ;主祭神 *[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] ;配神 *[[神武天皇]] *[[天照大神|天照大御神]] *豊岩窓命（北方神社：大正5年5月、阿賀神社：大正6年4月30日合祀） *[[大国主|大己貴命]]（小原神社：大正5年5月合祀） *[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]（小原神社：大正5年5月合祀） *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（田邊神社：大正6年4月30日合祀） *[[スサノオ|素戔嗚命]]（阿賀神社：大正6年4月30日合祀） == 境内 == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;境内神社 *糺神社 - 祭神：[[タマヨリビメ|玉依姫命]] Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 *鷺神社 - 祭神：稲背脛命、[[イワナガヒメ|磐長姫命]] Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == 現地情報 == === 所在地 === *[[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]倭3 === 交通アクセス === *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[山陰本線]][[米子駅]]より[[日ノ丸自動車]]バス法勝寺線「倭バス停」（約18分）下車後、徒歩約11分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|20em}} == 参考文献 == * {{Citation |和書 | last = 石破 | first = 洋 | year = 2000 | date = 2000年6月1日 | title = イナバノシロウサギの総合研究 | publisher = [[牧野出版]] }} * {{Cite web | author = 鳥取縣神社廰 | authorlink = | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = 賀茂神社 | website = 鳥取縣神社廰（公式ホームページ） | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |和書 | last = 鳥取県神職会 | first = | year = 1935 | title = 鳥取県神社誌 | publisher = 鳥取県神職会 | pages = 474–475 }} == 外部リンク == *[https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ 鳥取縣神社廰（公式ホームページ）：賀茂神社] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもしんしや}} [[Category:鳥取県の神社]] [[Category:鳥取県の旧郷社]] [[Category:鳥取県南部町の建築物]] [[Category:鳥取県南部町の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] to [[Kamo Shrine (Tottori Nanbu)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 賀茂神社 |画像 = [[File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]倭3 |位置 = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[大社造]] |別名 = 賀茂大明神
賀茂両社
賀茂下上大明神 |例祭 = [[10月19日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label=賀茂神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''賀茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は[[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]倭にある[[神社]]。 旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|郷社]]{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}。 == 由緒 == 創立年代不詳。京都[[賀茂別雷神社]]より[[分霊|御分霊]]を奉遷して大平山（おおなるやま）の頂上に鎮座していたと云われる。その後、同山の中腹小平という所に遷り、さらに同山の麓に奉遷したと伝えられる{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]、[[尼子経久]]が[[伯耆国]]を領有すると、崇敬篤く社領100石が寄附された他、度々参拝して祈願、奉幣、社殿造営が行われたが、[[尼子氏]]滅亡と共に当社も次第に衰微した{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}。[[江戸時代]]の[[1733年]]（[[享保]]18年）[[鳥取藩]]主[[池田氏#鳥取藩主家|池田氏]]より社領高10石の寄進があり、[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）4月郷社に列せられ、[[1907年]]（明治40年）[[2月3日]]に[[近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|神饌幣帛料供進社]]に指定された{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}。猪小路、北方、原、小原、新庄、上阿賀、下阿賀7ヶ村の氏神であり、近世まで賀茂大明神と称した{{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}。 [[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）5月に[[大国村 (鳥取県)|大国村]]大字北方字宮ノ峠ノ山鎮座の[[近代社格制度#諸社|無格社]]北方神社、同村大字原字客ノ前鎮座の無格社小原神社、[[1917年]]（大正6年）[[4月30日]]に[[天津村 (鳥取県)|天津村]]大字阿賀字王子﨏山鎮座の無格社阿賀神社、同村大字同字トヲベン神田鎮座の無格社田邊神社を[[神社合祀|合併]]した{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}。 === 境内社 === '''糺神社'''（ただすじんじゃ）は本社と同時の創立と思われ、本社と併せて'''賀茂両社'''、あるいは'''賀茂下上大明神'''と称されていた{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}。 '''鷺神社'''（さぎじんじゃ）の創立年代は不詳{{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}。もと[[雲伯]]の国境渡田峠、通称疱瘡ヶ峠と称する所に鎮座していた。古来[[疱瘡神]]として信仰篤く、旧藩主池田氏の祈祷所であったと伝えられる{{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}。[[1897年]]（明治30年）10月失火により社殿が焼失したが[[1905年]]（明治38年）5月に再建された。1916年（大正5年）猪小路鎮座の田中神社（祭神：[[イワナガヒメ|磐長姫命]]）を合併し、[[1917年]]（大正6年）に渡田峠より現在地の賀茂神社境内に奉遷した{{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}。 もとの場所には小祠があり祠内に疱瘡地蔵が祀られ{{Sfn|石破|2000|p=30}}、小祠の前には[[1825年]]（[[文政]]8年）6月の御神燈が残されている{{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}。現在の祭神は稲背脛命であるが『伯耆志』に「祭神は[[中山神社_(鳥取県大山町)#境内社|束積の鷺神社]]に同じ」とあるため、もとの祭神は[[スサノオ]]であった可能性がある{{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}。 == 祭神 == ;主祭神 *[[賀茂別雷命|別雷命]] ;配神 *[[神武天皇]] *[[天照大神|天照大御神]] *豊岩窓命（北方神社：大正5年5月、阿賀神社：大正6年4月30日合祀） *[[大国主|大己貴命]]（小原神社：大正5年5月合祀） *[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]（小原神社：大正5年5月合祀） *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（田邊神社：大正6年4月30日合祀） *[[スサノオ|素戔嗚命]]（阿賀神社：大正6年4月30日合祀） == 境内 == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;境内神社 *糺神社 - 祭神：[[タマヨリビメ|玉依姫命]] Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 *鷺神社 - 祭神：稲背脛命、[[イワナガヒメ|磐長姫命]] Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == 現地情報 == === 所在地 === *[[鳥取県]][[西伯郡]][[南部町 (鳥取県)|南部町]]倭3 === 交通アクセス === *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[山陰本線]][[米子駅]]より[[日ノ丸自動車]]バス法勝寺線「倭バス停」（約18分）下車後、徒歩約11分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|20em}} == 参考文献 == * {{Citation |和書 | last = 石破 | first = 洋 | year = 2000 | date = 2000年6月1日 | title = イナバノシロウサギの総合研究 | publisher = [[牧野出版]] }} * {{Cite web | author = 鳥取縣神社廰 | authorlink = | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = 賀茂神社 | website = 鳥取縣神社廰（公式ホームページ） | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |和書 | last = 鳥取県神職会 | first = | year = 1935 | title = 鳥取県神社誌 | publisher = 鳥取県神職会 | pages = 474–475 }} == 外部リンク == *[https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ 鳥取縣神社廰（公式ホームページ）：賀茂神社] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもしんしや}} [[Category:鳥取県の神社]] [[Category:鳥取県の旧郷社]] [[Category:鳥取県南部町の建築物]] [[Category:鳥取県南部町の歴史]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|12=draft|12=draft}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=draft|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊|12=draft|12=draft}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|12=draft|12=draft}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=draft|12=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=draft|12=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|12=draft|12=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=draft|12=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=draft|12=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|12=draft|12=draft}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=draft|12=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|12=draft|12=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=draft|12=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ|lt=Tamayoribime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|12=draft|12=draft}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|ja|米子駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|12=draft|12=draft}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|12=draft|12=draft}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|12=draft|12=draft}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)|version=103196017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ|lt=Tamayoribime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|ja|米子駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社

Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ|lt=Tamayoribime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|ja|米子駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)|version=103196017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha of Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Former Gō-sha of " to "Former Gō-sha in "
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|賀茂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Branch Spirit|ja|分霊|lt=Branch Spirit|lt_ja=御分霊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamo Betsurai Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|lt=Sengoku period|lt_ja=戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|ja|伯耆国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|ja|尼子氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|ja|鳥取藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|ja|2月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokuhō|ja|北方|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hara|ja|原|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kobara|ja|小原|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|ja|4月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Taishō era|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Merger|ja|神社合祀|lt=Shrine Merger|lt_ja=合併|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox God|ja|疱瘡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|ja|1905th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|ja|1916th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|lt=Amaterasu|lt_ja=天照大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|スクナビコナ|lt=Sukunabikona|lt_ja=少彦名命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama|lt_ja=倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ|lt=Tamayoribime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|lt_ja=磐長姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Saiboku District|ja|西伯郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nanbu Town|ja|南部町 (鳥取県)|lt=Nanbu Town|lt_ja=南部町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|ja|米子駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)|version=103196017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nanbu Town, Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]]
