Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = [[:en:October 19|October 19]] |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label=[[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nanbu|qid=Q1156169}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.349833333333336|N|133.33183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a [[:en:Bunrei|Branch Spirit]] was transferred from Kyoto’s [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Betsurai Shrine]] and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], when [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] controlled [[:en:Hōki Province|Hōki Province]], the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the [[:en:Amago clan|Amago clan]], the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:1733|1733]] ([[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18), the lord of the [[:en:Tottori Domain|Tottori Domain]], {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|qq=|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on [[:en:February 3|February 3]] [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kitagata|Hokuhō]], [[:en:Hara|Hara]], [[:en:Ohara|Kobara]], {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshō|qq=|1=Shinshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113636829}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. It had long been venerated as the [[:en:Smallpox demon|Smallpox God]], and served as a prayer site for the former lord [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May [[:en:1905|1905]] (Meiji 38). In [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Shrine|qq=|1=Tanaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575776}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining deity: [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto]]) was merged, and in [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} ;Accompanying Deities * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505771}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505774}}: merged May Taishō 5) * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505774}}: merged May Taishō 5) * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama]] (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505777}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505779}} – Enshrined Deity: [[:en:Tamayori-hime|Tamayoribime]] Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505781}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto]] Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] on the [[:en:San'in Main Line|Sanin Main Line]] at [[:en:Yonago Station|Yonago Station]], take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomarudōsha|qq=|1=Hinomaru Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504700}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Publishing|qq=|1=Makino Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019558}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Shrine Administration|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)|version=103196017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Tottori Nanbu)]]}} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Nanbu Town (Tottori)]] [[Category:Municipal History of Southern Tottori]] {{Commons category|Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)}} [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Tottori Nanbu)]] {{wikidata link|Q131584091}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q131584091]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q131584091]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = [[:en:October 19|October 19]] |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label=[[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nanbu|qid=Q1156169}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.349833333333336|N|133.33183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a [[:en:Bunrei|Branch Spirit]] was transferred from Kyoto’s [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Betsurai Shrine]] and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], when [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] controlled [[:en:Hōki Province|Hōki Province]], the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the [[:en:Amago clan|Amago clan]], the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:1733|1733]] ([[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18), the lord of the [[:en:Tottori Domain|Tottori Domain]], {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|qq=|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on [[:en:February 3|February 3]] [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kitagata|Hokuhō]], [[:en:Hara|Hara]], [[:en:Ohara|Kobara]], {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshō|qq=|1=Shinshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113636829}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May [[:en:1916|1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505771}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni Village|qq=|1=ookunison|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485639}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505774}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] [[:en:1917|1917]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin Village|qq=|1=Tenshin Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485638}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505777}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Merger]] {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505779}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Ryōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505781}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unpaku|qq=|1=Unpaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660010}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the [[:en:Smallpox demon|Smallpox God]], and served as a prayer site for the former lord [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May [[:en:1905|1905]] (Meiji 38). In [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Shrine|qq=|1=Tanaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575776}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining deity: [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto]]) was merged, and in [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} ;Accompanying Deities * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505771}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505774}}: merged May Taishō 5) * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505774}}: merged May Taishō 5) * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama]] (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505777}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505779}} – Enshrined Deity: [[:en:Tamayori-hime|Tamayoribime]] Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505781}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto]] Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] on the [[:en:San'in Main Line|Sanin Main Line]] at [[:en:Yonago Station|Yonago Station]], take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomarudōsha|qq=|1=Hinomaru Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504700}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Publishing|qq=|1=Makino Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019558}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Shrine Administration|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)|version=103196017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Nanbu Town (Tottori)]] [[Category:Municipal History of Southern Tottori]] {{Commons category|Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)}} [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q131584091}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q131584091]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q131584091]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = [[:en:October 19|October 19]] |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label=[[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nanbu|qid=Q1156169}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.349833333333336|N|133.33183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a [[:en:Bunrei|Branch Spirit]] was transferred from Kyoto’s [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Betsurai Shrine]] and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], when [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] controlled [[:en:Hōki Province|Hōki Province]], the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the [[:en:Amago clan|Amago clan]], the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:1733|1733]] ([[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18), the lord of the [[:en:Tottori Domain|Tottori Domain]], {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|qq=|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on [[:en:February 3|February 3]] [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kitagata|Hokuhō]], [[:en:Hara|Hara]], [[:en:Ohara|Kobara]], {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshō|qq=|1=Shinshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113636829}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May [[:en:1916|1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505771}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni Village|qq=|1=ookunison|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485639}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505774}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] [[:en:1917|1917]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin Village|qq=|1=Tenshin Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485638}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505777}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Merger]] {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505779}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Ryōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505781}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unpaku|qq=|1=Unpaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660010}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the [[:en:Smallpox demon|Smallpox God]], and served as a prayer site for the former lord [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May [[:en:1905|1905]] (Meiji 38). In [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Shrine|qq=|1=Tanaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575776}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining deity: [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto]]) was merged, and in [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} ;Accompanying Deities * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505771}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505774}}: merged May Taishō 5) * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505774}}: merged May Taishō 5) * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama]] (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505777}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505779}} – Enshrined Deity: [[:en:Tamayori-hime|Tamayoribime]] Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505781}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto]] Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] on the [[:en:San'in Main Line|Sanin Main Line]] at [[:en:Yonago Station|Yonago Station]], take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomarudōsha|qq=|1=Hinomaru Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504700}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Publishing|qq=|1=Makino Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019558}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Shrine Administration|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)|version=103196017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Nanbu Town (Tottori)]] [[Category:Municipal History of Southern Tottori]] {{Commons category|Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q131584091}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q131584091]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q131584091]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = [[:en:October 19|October 19]] |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label=[[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nanbu|qid=Q1156169}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.349833333333336|N|133.33183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a [[:en:Bunrei|Branch Spirit]] was transferred from Kyoto’s [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Betsurai Shrine]] and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], when [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] controlled [[:en:Hōki Province|Hōki Province]], the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the [[:en:Amago clan|Amago clan]], the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:1733|1733]] ([[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18), the lord of the [[:en:Tottori Domain|Tottori Domain]], {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|qq=|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on [[:en:February 3|February 3]] [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kitagata|Hokuhō]], [[:en:Hara|Hara]], [[:en:Ohara|Kobara]], {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshō|qq=|1=Shinshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113636829}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May [[:en:1916|1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505771}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni Village|qq=|1=ookunison|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485639}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505774}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] [[:en:1917|1917]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin Village|qq=|1=Tenshin Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485638}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505777}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Merger]] {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505779}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Ryōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505781}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unpaku|qq=|1=Unpaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660010}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the [[:en:Smallpox demon|Smallpox God]], and served as a prayer site for the former lord [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May [[:en:1905|1905]] (Meiji 38). In [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Shrine|qq=|1=Tanaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575776}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining deity: [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto]]) was merged, and in [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} ;Accompanying Deities * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505771}}: May Taishō 5; [[commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q131584091]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q131584091]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = [[:en:October 19|October 19]] |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label=[[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nanbu|qid=Q1156169}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.349833333333336|N|133.33183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a [[:en:Bunrei|Branch Spirit]] was transferred from Kyoto’s [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Betsurai Shrine]] and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], when [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] controlled [[:en:Hōki Province|Hōki Province]], the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the [[:en:Amago clan|Amago clan]], the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:1733|1733]] ([[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18), the lord of the [[:en:Tottori Domain|Tottori Domain]], {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|qq=|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on [[:en:February 3|February 3]] [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kitagata|Hokuhō]], [[:en:Hara|Hara]], [[:en:Ohara|Kobara]], {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshō|qq=|1=Shinshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113636829}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May [[:en:1916|1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505771}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni Village|qq=|1=ookunison|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485639}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505774}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] [[:en:1917|1917]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 In [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Shrine|qq=|1=Tanaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575776}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining deity: [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto]]) was merged, and in [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} ;Accompanying Deities * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505771}}: May Taishō 5; 右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505779}} – Enshrined Deity: [[:en:Tamayori-hime|Tamayoribime]] Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505781}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto]] Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] on the [[:en:San'in Main Line|Sanin Main Line]] at [[:en:Yonago Station|Yonago Station]], take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomarudōsha|qq=|1=Hinomaru Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504700}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Publishing|qq=|1=Makino Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019558}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Shrine Administration|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)|version=103196017|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Nanbu Town (Tottori)]] [[Category:Municipal History of Southern Tottori]] {{Commons category|Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)}} {{wikidata link|Q131584091}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q131584091]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q131584091]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = [[:en:October 19|October 19]] |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label=[[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nanbu|qid=Q1156169}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.349833333333336|N|133.33183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a [[:en:Bunrei|Branch Spirit]] was transferred from Kyoto’s [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Betsurai Shrine]] and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], when [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] controlled [[:en:Hōki Province|Hōki Province]], the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the [[:en:Amago clan|Amago clan]], the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:1733|1733]] ([[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18), the lord of the [[:en:Tottori Domain|Tottori Domain]], {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|qq=|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on [[:en:February 3|February 3]] [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kitagata|Hokuhō]], [[:en:Hara|Hara]], [[:en:Ohara|Kobara]], {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshō|qq=|1=Shinshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113636829}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May [[:en:1916|1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505771}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni Village|qq=|1=ookunison|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485639}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505774}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] [[:en:1917|1917]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin Village|qq=|1=Tenshin Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485638}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505777}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Merger]] {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505779}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Ryōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505781}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unpaku|qq=|1=Unpaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660010}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the [[:en:Smallpox demon|Smallpox God]], and served as a prayer site for the former lord [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May [[:en:1905|1905]] (Meiji 38). In [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Shrine|qq=|1=Tanaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575776}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining deity: [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto]]) was merged, and in [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} ;Accompanying Deities * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505771}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505774}}: merged May Taishō 5) * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505774}}: merged May Taishō 5) * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama]] (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505777}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社

[[sn:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q131584091]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q131584091]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]] |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = [[:en:October 19|October 19]] |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label=[[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]]|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nanbu|WD=Q1156169}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.349833333333336|N|133.33183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Saihaku District, Tottori|Saiboku District]] [[:en:Nanbu, Tottori|Nanbu Town]] Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a [[:en:Bunrei|Branch Spirit]] was transferred from Kyoto’s [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Betsurai Shrine]] and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], when [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] controlled [[:en:Hōki Province|Hōki Province]], the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the [[:en:Amago clan|Amago clan]], the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:1733|1733]] ([[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18), the lord of the [[:en:Tottori Domain|Tottori Domain]], {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|qq=|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on [[:en:February 3|February 3]] [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kitagata|Hokuhō]], [[:en:Hara|Hara]], [[:en:Ohara|Kobara]], {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshō|qq=|1=Shinshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113636829}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May [[:en:1916|1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505771}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni Village|qq=|1=ookunison|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485639}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] [[:en:1917|1917]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin Village|qq=|1=Tenshin Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485638}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505777}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Merger]] {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505779}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Ryōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505781}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unpaku|qq=|1=Unpaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660010}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the [[:en:Smallpox demon|Smallpox God]], and served as a prayer site for the former lord [[:en:Ikeda clan|Ikeda clan]] {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October [[:en:1897|1897]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May [[:en:1905|1905]] (Meiji 38). In [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Shrine|qq=|1=Tanaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575776}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining deity: [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime-no-mikoto]]) was merged, and in [[:en:1917|1917]] (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} ;Accompanying Deities * [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]] * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505771}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774}}: merged May Taishō 5) * [[:en:Sukunabikona|Sukunabikona]] (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774}}: merged May Taishō 5) * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama]] (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505777}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社

Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a [[:en:Bunrei|Branch Spirit]] was transferred from Kyoto’s [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamo Betsurai Shrine]] and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], when [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] controlled [[:en:Hōki Province|Hōki Province]], the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the [[:en:Amago clan|Amago clan]], the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:1733|1733]] ([[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 18), the lord of the [[:en:Tottori Domain|Tottori Domain]], {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|qq=|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In [[:en:1872|1872]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on [[:en:February 3|February 3]] [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|qq=|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kitagata|Hokuhō]], [[:en:Hara|Hara]], [[:en:Ohara|Kobara]], {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshō|qq=|1=Shinshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113636829}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Aga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May [[:en:1916|1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505771}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni Village|qq=|1=ookunison|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485639}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on [[:en:April 30|April 30]] [[:en:1917|1917]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村
