revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Saihaku District, Tottori|en|Saihaku District, Tottori|WD=Q917024|lt=Saiboku District}} {{ill|Nanbu, Tottori|en|Nanbu, Tottori|WD=Q1156169|lt=Nanbu Town}} 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|en|Taisha-zukuri|WD=Q387874|lt=Taisha-zukuri}} |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|en|October 19|WD=Q2961|lt=October 19}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nanbu|WD=Q1156169}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.349833333333336|N|133.33183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Saihaku District, Tottori|en|Saihaku District, Tottori|WD=Q917024|lt=Saiboku District}} {{ill|Nanbu, Tottori|en|Nanbu, Tottori|WD=Q1156169|lt=Nanbu Town}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Branch Spirit}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Betsurai Shrine}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|en|Amago Tsunehisa|WD=Q1045252|lt=Amago Tsunehisa}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|en|Hōki Province|WD=Q1053703|lt=Hōki Province}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|en|Amago clan|WD=Q2575609|lt=Amago clan}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, in {{ill|1733|en|1733|WD=Q6972|lt=1733}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|en|February 3|WD=Q2315|lt=February 3}} {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kitagata|en|Kitagata|WD=Q1296670|lt=Hokuhō}}, {{ill|Hara|en|Hara|WD=Q349870|lt=Hara}}, {{ill|Ohara|en|Ohara|WD=Q3881286|lt=Kobara}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshō|1=Shinshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113636829}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Aga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Aga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505771}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni Village|1=ookunison|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485639}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|en|April 30|WD=Q2536|lt=April 30}} {{ill|1917|en|1917|WD=Q2092|lt=1917}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin Village|1=Tenshin Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485638}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505777}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|en|Shrine Consolidation Policy|WD=Q11590700|lt=Shrine Merger}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505779}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Ryōsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505781}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unpaku|1=Unpaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660010}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|WD=Q7543689|lt=Smallpox God}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|en|1905|WD=Q2047|lt=1905}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Shrine|1=Tanaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575776}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|en|1917|WD=Q2092|lt=1917}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505771}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505777}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505779}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505781}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Saihaku District, Tottori|en|Saihaku District, Tottori|WD=Q917024|lt=Saiboku District}} {{ill|Nanbu, Tottori|en|Nanbu, Tottori|WD=Q1156169|lt=Nanbu Town}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} on the {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=Sanin Main Line}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|en|Yonago Station|WD=Q1139558|lt=Yonago Station}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomarudōsha|1=Hinomaru Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504700}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Publishing|1=Makino Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019558}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Shrine Administration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)|version=103196017|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]] {{Commons category|Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)}} {{wikidata link|Q131584091}} [[Category:Architecture of Nanbu Town (Tottori)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Southern Tottori]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Saihaku District, Tottori|en|Saihaku District, Tottori|WD=Q917024|lt=Saiboku District|1=Saihaku District, Tottori}} {{ill|Nanbu, Tottori|en|Nanbu, Tottori|WD=Q1156169|lt=Nanbu Town|1=Nanbu, Tottori}} 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|en|Taisha-zukuri|WD=Q387874|lt=Taisha-zukuri|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|en|October 19|WD=Q2961|lt=October 19|1=October 19}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo shrines}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nanbu|WD=Q1156169|1=Nanbu, Tottori}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.349833333333336|N|133.33183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Saihaku District, Tottori|en|Saihaku District, Tottori|WD=Q917024|lt=Saiboku District|1=Saihaku District, Tottori}} {{ill|Nanbu, Tottori|en|Nanbu, Tottori|WD=Q1156169|lt=Nanbu Town|1=Nanbu, Tottori}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Branch Spirit|1=Bunrei}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Betsurai Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|en|Amago Tsunehisa|WD=Q1045252|lt=Amago Tsunehisa|1=Amago Tsunehisa}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|en|Hōki Province|WD=Q1053703|lt=Hōki Province|1=Hōki Province}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|en|Amago clan|WD=Q2575609|lt=Amago clan|1=Amago clan}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, in {{ill|1733|en|1733|WD=Q6972|lt=1733|1=1733}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|en|February 3|WD=Q2315|lt=February 3|1=February 3}} {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kitagata|en|Kitagata|WD=Q1296670|lt=Hokuhō|1=Kitagata}}, {{ill|Hara|en|Hara|WD=Q349870|lt=Hara|1=Hara}}, {{ill|Ohara|en|Ohara|WD=Q3881286|lt=Kobara|1=Ohara}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshō|1=Q113636829|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113636829}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Aga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Aga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505771|1=Hokuhō Shrine}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni Village|1=ookunison|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485639}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774|1=Kobara Shrine}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|en|April 30|WD=Q2536|lt=April 30|1=April 30}} {{ill|1917|en|1917|WD=Q2092|lt=1917|1=1917}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin Village|1=Tenshin Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485638}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505777|1=Tanabe Shrine}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|en|Shrine Consolidation Policy|WD=Q11590700|lt=Shrine Merger|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505779|1=Tadasu Shrine}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Ryōsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505781|1=Sagi Shrine}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unpaku|1=Unpaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660010}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|WD=Q7543689|lt=Smallpox God|1=Smallpox demon}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|en|1905|WD=Q2047|lt=1905|1=1905}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Shrine|1=Tanaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575776}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|1=Iwanaga-hime}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|en|1917|WD=Q2092|lt=1917|1=1917}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu|1=Amaterasu}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505771|1=Hokuhō Shrine}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774|1=Kobara Shrine}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774|1=Kobara Shrine}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama|1=Ukanomitama}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505777|1=Tanabe Shrine}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社
右が糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii 2.jpg|二の鳥居 ;Shrines within the precincts * {{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505779|1=Tadasu Shrine}} – Enshrined Deity: {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime|1=Tamayori-hime}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Keidai.jpg|糺神社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Tadasu Shrine.jpg|拝殿と本殿 * {{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505781|1=Sagi Shrine}} – Enshrined Deities: Inabashikibi-no-mikoto, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|1=Iwanaga-hime}} Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Sagi Shrine.jpg|鷺神社 == Local Information == === Location === * {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Saihaku District, Tottori|en|Saihaku District, Tottori|WD=Q917024|lt=Saiboku District|1=Saihaku District, Tottori}} {{ill|Nanbu, Tottori|en|Nanbu, Tottori|WD=Q1156169|lt=Nanbu Town|1=Nanbu, Tottori}} Yamato 3 === Transportation Access === * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} on the {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=Sanin Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}} at {{ill|Yonago Station|en|Yonago Station|WD=Q1139558|lt=Yonago Station|1=Yonago Station}}, take the {{ill|Hinomarudōsha|ja|日ノ丸自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinomarudōsha|1=Hinomaru Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504700}} bus on the Hōshōji Line to “Yamato Bus Stop” (approximately 18 minutes). Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Publishing|1=Makino Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019558}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Shrine Administration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)|version=103196017|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]] {{Commons category|Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)}} {{wikidata link|Q131584091}} [[Category:Architecture of Nanbu Town (Tottori)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Southern Tottori]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[Category:sn_crud commons:Category:Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)]][[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kamo Shrine |image = File:Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine.jpg |Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Saihaku District, Tottori|en|Saihaku District, Tottori|WD=Q917024|lt=Saiboku District|1=Saihaku District, Tottori}} {{ill|Nanbu, Tottori|en|Nanbu, Tottori|WD=Q1156169|lt=Nanbu Town|1=Nanbu, Tottori}} 倭3 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|en|Taisha-zukuri|WD=Q387874|lt=Taisha-zukuri|1=Taisha-zukuri}} |Other Names = Kamo Daimyōjin
Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|en|October 19|WD=Q2961|lt=October 19|1=October 19}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo shrines}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nanbu|WD=Q1156169|1=Nanbu, Tottori}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.349833333333336|N|133.33183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Saihaku District, Tottori|en|Saihaku District, Tottori|WD=Q917024|lt=Saiboku District|1=Saihaku District, Tottori}} {{ill|Nanbu, Tottori|en|Nanbu, Tottori|WD=Q1156169|lt=Nanbu Town|1=Nanbu, Tottori}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Branch Spirit|1=Bunrei}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Betsurai Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|en|Amago Tsunehisa|WD=Q1045252|lt=Amago Tsunehisa|1=Amago Tsunehisa}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|en|Hōki Province|WD=Q1053703|lt=Hōki Province|1=Hōki Province}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|en|Amago clan|WD=Q2575609|lt=Amago clan|1=Amago clan}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, in {{ill|1733|en|1733|WD=Q6972|lt=1733|1=1733}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|en|February 3|WD=Q2315|lt=February 3|1=February 3}} {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kitagata|en|Kitagata|WD=Q1296670|lt=Hokuhō|1=Kitagata}}, {{ill|Hara|en|Hara|WD=Q349870|lt=Hara|1=Hara}}, {{ill|Ohara|en|Ohara|WD=Q3881286|lt=Kobara|1=Ohara}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshō|1=Q113636829|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113636829}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Aga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Aga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505771|1=Hokuhō Shrine}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Village|ja|大国村 (鳥取県)|lt=Ōkuni Village|lt_ja=大国村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuni Village|1=ookunison|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485639}}—and the unranked {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774|1=Kobara Shrine}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|en|April 30|WD=Q2536|lt=April 30|1=April 30}} {{ill|1917|en|1917|WD=Q2092|lt=1917|1=1917}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin Village|1=Tenshin Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485638}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505777|1=Tanabe Shrine}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|en|Shrine Consolidation Policy|WD=Q11590700|lt=Shrine Merger|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505779|1=Tadasu Shrine}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Ryōsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505781|1=Sagi Shrine}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unpaku|1=Unpaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660010}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|WD=Q7543689|lt=Smallpox God|1=Smallpox demon}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|en|1905|WD=Q2047|lt=1905|1=1905}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Shrine|1=Tanaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575776}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|1=Iwanaga-hime}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|en|1917|WD=Q2092|lt=1917|1=1917}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu|1=Amaterasu}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505771|1=Hokuhō Shrine}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774|1=Kobara Shrine}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774|1=Kobara Shrine}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama|1=Ukanomitama}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505777|1=Tanabe Shrine}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社

Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Publishing|1=Makino Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019558}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Shrine Administration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)|version=103196017|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]] {{Commons category|Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)}} {{wikidata link|Q131584091}} [[Category:Architecture of Nanbu Town (Tottori)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Southern Tottori]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|en|October 19|WD=Q2961|lt=October 19|1=October 19}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo shrines}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nanbu|WD=Q1156169|1=Nanbu, Tottori}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.349833333333336|N|133.33183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Saihaku District, Tottori|en|Saihaku District, Tottori|WD=Q917024|lt=Saiboku District|1=Saihaku District, Tottori}} {{ill|Nanbu, Tottori|en|Nanbu, Tottori|WD=Q1156169|lt=Nanbu Town|1=Nanbu, Tottori}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505771|1=Hokuhō Shrine}}—enshrined at Miyano-Tōge-yama in the Hokuhō district of {{ill|Ōkuni Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774|1=Kobara Shrine}}—enshrined at Kyakuno Mae in the same village—were merged with, on {{ill|April 30|en|April 30|WD=Q2536|lt=April 30|1=April 30}} {{ill|1917|en|1917|WD=Q2092|lt=1917|1=1917}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 6), the unranked {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}} (enshrined at Oga, Oji Kiriyama in the large district of Aga in {{ill|Tenshin Village|ja|天津村 (鳥取県)|lt=Tenshin Village|lt_ja=天津村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin Village|1=Tenshin Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105485638}}) and the unranked {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505777|1=Tanabe Shrine}} (enshrined at Towoben Kandō in the same village) through a {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|en|Shrine Consolidation Policy|WD=Q11590700|lt=Shrine Merger|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. === Shrine within the precincts === '''{{ill|Tadasu Shrine|ja|糺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505779|1=Tadasu Shrine}}''' (ただすじんじゃ) is believed to have been founded at the same time as the main shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Ryōsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂下上大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. '''{{ill|Sagi Shrine|ja|鷺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505781|1=Sagi Shrine}}''' (さぎじんじゃ) has an unknown founding date {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. It was originally enshrined at Watada Pass on the border of {{ill|Unpaku|ja|雲伯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unpaku|1=Unpaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660010}}—a location commonly known as “Smallpox Pass”. It had long been venerated as the {{ill|Smallpox demon|en|Smallpox demon|WD=Q7543689|lt=Smallpox God|1=Smallpox demon}}, and served as a prayer site for the former lord {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|WD=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. In October {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 30) the shrine building was destroyed by fire, but it was rebuilt in May {{ill|1905|en|1905|WD=Q2047|lt=1905|1=1905}} (Meiji 38). In {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5) the {{ill|Tanaka Shrine|ja|田中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Shrine|1=Tanaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575776}} of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining deity: {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|WD=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime-no-mikoto|1=Iwanaga-hime}}) was merged, and in {{ill|1917|en|1917|WD=Q2092|lt=1917|1=1917}} (Taishō 6) it was transferred from Watada Pass to the current precincts of Kamo Shrine {{Sfn|石破|2000|p=31}}. == Enshrined Deities == ;Main Deity * {{ill|Betsurai-no-mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|lt=Betsurai-no-mikoto|lt_ja=別雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} ;Accompanying Deities * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu|1=Amaterasu}} * Toyoiwato-no-mikoto (at {{ill|Hokuhō Shrine|ja|北方神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokuhō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505771|1=Hokuhō Shrine}}: May Taishō 5; at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774|1=Kobara Shrine}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}} (at {{ill|Kobara Shrine|ja|小原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505774|1=Kobara Shrine}}: merged May Taishō 5) * {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama|1=Ukanomitama}} (at {{ill|Tanabe Shrine|ja|田邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505777|1=Tanabe Shrine}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (at {{ill|Aga Shrine|ja|阿賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aga Shrine|qq=}}: merged April 30, Taishō 6) == Precincts == Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Honden.jpg|本殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Kagura-den.jpg|神楽殿 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Saikiko.jpg|祭器庫 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Chozuya.jpg|手水社 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Torii.jpg|一の鳥居 Tottori Nanbu-cho Yamato Kamo Shrine Sando.jpg|左が賀茂神社

Then walk for about 11 minutes. == Footnotes == {{Reflist|20em}} == References == * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Ishiba | first = Hiro | year = 2000 | date = June 1, 2000 | title = Comprehensive Study of the Inabano Shiro Usagi | publisher = {{ill|Makino Publishing|ja|牧野出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makino Publishing|1=Makino Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21019558}} }} * {{Cite web | author = {{ill|Tottori Shrine Administration|ja|鳥取縣神社廰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Shrine Administration|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | url = https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ | title = Kamo Shrine | website = Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage) | year = 2024 | accessdate = 2024-12-24 | ref = {{SfnRef|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}} }} * {{Citation |Book (Japanese) | last = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | year = 1935 | title = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Gazette | publisher = Tottori Prefecture Shrine Clergy Association | pages = 474–475 }} == External Links == * [https://tottori-jinjacho.jp/pages/551/ Tottori Shrine Administration (Official Homepage): Kamo Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)|version=103196017|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:賀茂神社 (鳥取県南部町)]] [[ja:賀茂神社]] {{Commons category|Kamo-jinja (Nanbu, Tottori)}} {{wikidata link|Q131584091}} [[Category:Architecture of Nanbu Town (Tottori)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Southern Tottori]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Kamo Ryōsha
Kamo Shimokami Daimyōjin |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 19|en|October 19|WD=Q2961|lt=October 19|1=October 19}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tottori|label={{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo shrines}}|coordinates= {{coord2|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|59.4|N|133|19|54.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nanbu|WD=Q1156169|1=Nanbu, Tottori}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.349833333333336|N|133.33183333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (賀茂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Saihaku District, Tottori|en|Saihaku District, Tottori|WD=Q917024|lt=Saiboku District|1=Saihaku District, Tottori}} {{ill|Nanbu, Tottori|en|Nanbu, Tottori|WD=Q1156169|lt=Nanbu Town|1=Nanbu, Tottori}} Yamato. Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Gōsha|lt_ja=郷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. == History == The founding date is unknown. It is said that a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Branch Spirit|1=Bunrei}} was transferred from Kyoto’s {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Betsurai Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} and enshrined at the summit of Ōnaru-yama (大平山). Later it was moved to a place called Kobira on the mid-slope of the same mountain, and finally transferred to the foot of the mountain {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, when {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|en|Amago Tsunehisa|WD=Q1045252|lt=Amago Tsunehisa|1=Amago Tsunehisa}} controlled {{ill|Hōki Province|en|Hōki Province|WD=Q1053703|lt=Hōki Province|1=Hōki Province}}, the shrine was deeply revered; not only was an offering equivalent to 100 koku donated, but frequent visits, prayers, offerings, and shrine building were performed. However, with the downfall of the {{ill|Amago clan|en|Amago clan|WD=Q2575609|lt=Amago clan|1=Amago clan}}, the shrine gradually declined {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=474}}. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, in {{ill|1733|en|1733|WD=Q6972|lt=1733|1=1733}} ({{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 18), the lord of the {{ill|Tottori Domain|en|Tottori Domain|WD=Q905521|lt=Tottori Domain|1=Tottori Domain}}, {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田氏#鳥取藩主家|lt=Ikeda clan|lt_ja=池田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}}, donated an additional 10 koku. In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 5) in April it was classified as a Gōsha, and on {{ill|February 3|en|February 3|WD=Q2315|lt=February 3|1=February 3}} {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) it was designated as a {{ill|Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsenheihaku Rikyō Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} {{Sfn|鳥取県神職会|1935|p=475}}. It served as the tutelary shrine for the seven villages of {{ill|Inokoji|ja|猪小路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inokoji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kitagata|en|Kitagata|WD=Q1296670|lt=Hokuhō|1=Kitagata}}, {{ill|Hara|en|Hara|WD=Q349870|lt=Hara|1=Hara}}, {{ill|Ohara|en|Ohara|WD=Q3881286|lt=Kobara|1=Ohara}}, {{ill|Shinshō|ja|新庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinshō|1=Q113636829|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113636829}}, {{ill|Kami-Aga|ja|上阿賀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Aga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimo-Aga|ja|下阿賀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Aga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and was known as {{ill|Kamo Daimyōjin|ja|賀茂大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} until the early modern period {{Sfn|鳥取縣神社廰|2024}}. In May {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 5), the unranked {{ill|Hokuhō shrine, and together they were referred to as either '''{{ill|Kamo Ryōsha|ja|賀茂両社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Ryōsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' or '''{{ill|Kamo Shimokami
